reason: Prone is inherently probabilistic (SoldierCombat.prone_chance caps at 60% per qualifying strike, gated on impulse exceeding a mass-scaled threshold), not a deterministic per-tick event -- a scripted scenario can stage a clash but can't guarantee a prone event actually fires within a short recorded window. Tried a Cavalry-vs-Infantry clash (demos/inputs -- reverted) verified via dump-state.sh across ticks 10-220 at multiple densities; zero prone events occurred in that run, and reliably forcing one would need either a much longer recording or directly seeding _sim_prone, neither of which fits a short demo clip. The fix itself (a soldier's mark easing into its fallen pose over ~0.25s instead of snapping) is directly and deterministically verified instead by GUT tests that set Unit._sim_prone directly and assert _render_prone_progress ramps gradually across ticks: test_falling_prone_eases_over_several_ticks_instead_of_snapping, test_rising_from_prone_also_eases_instead_of_snapping, plus the interpolation tests for _soldier_render_color/_facing_pip_transform at intermediate progress values.
